item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Snow days suck as an adult
item.description: I live in the largest city in Alaska. The past couple of winters the city had record snowfall that resulted in the entire city shutting down for multiple days at a time because it was physically impossible for most vehicles to drive anywhere. This was due in part to the mayor and governor both selling off most of the snow removal vehicles to pay for their own pet projects. The mayor then also fired most of the workers who knew how to operate the remaining snow plows and put his unqualified buddies in charge of snow removal. So when the plows DID actually come thru they did a piss poor job of plowing the streets and then left them in that state for weeks at a time. The main roads would eventually become drivable, but most of the side streets (i.e.: neighborhoods) remained so badly plowed that many of them were reduced to a single lane. After 2 years of this in a row the mayor was finally kicked out of office this year (partly because he also kept doing various illegal crap like stealing funds from other projects to try to fund his own project).
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: Wednesday WHOLESOME dump that will brighten your day (if it makes you smile, OP will do a push up)
item.description:
1. May we all retain such sense of wonderment and curiosity
2. He won’t take treats until he knows his sister got one...
3. Legolas getting on the Mumakill be like
4. Archer Jennifer Delaney shoots a mint in midair
5
6. Kitten after a hard day of chillin
7
8. This animal literally has the word "CHILL" in it's name
9. You are my sunshine, my only sunshine
10. Guys, guys the human is here
11
12. Goat tippy taps
13. Let me show how it's done, little fella
14. Gonna bury this for winter
15. Hello friendos, for the past year and a half, I’ve been working on a game that’s very close to my heart. "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure crafted with love and passion, with my brother by my side, helping every step of the way. It was inspired by games like The Stanley Parable. As indie developers, we rely on your support to bring our game to life. By adding it to your wishlist, you’re helping us reach more players and make a big impact in the gaming community. https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ We can’t do this without you! Thank you for supporting our dream and helping us share our wild adventure with the world!

item.title: another victim of capitalism
item.dc:creator: pilomotor

item.title: Freaking finally!!
item.description: I’ve been on the job hunt for months, and I finally got a bite with an interview!! Got the call last week that they accepted me!! Just gotta get through background checks, so as long as I’m not a criminal, I’ll be a working stuff again. Edit: Thanks everyone for the kind words! I’m exciting to get back to working. Not working was nice for a little bit, but I felt I wasn’t pulling my weight at home. I feel much better about all of it now

item.title: I painted this oil painting Brett Hart!
item.description: Both me and my client love how it came out! :) If this is the first time you come across me, my name is Anis Jerbi and I'm a 21-year-old artist studying at the fine arts academy of Verona so if you'd like to support me as a young artist, please make sure to follow me. It helps my art reach more people and therefore, more potential customers which helps me make that good old $$$$$$. :) Also, I would like to give a special thanks to all of you who donate to support me either via Patreon with monthly donations or via PayPal with one-time donations. Link to my Patreon: Patreon.com/anis_jerbi_art Link to my PayPal: PayPal.me/anisjerbi If you'd like to see all of my artworks, you can see them all on my official website: anisjerbi.com. If you'd like to purchase on of my artworks or commission an artwork from me, you can let me know by writing a comment, sending me a dm or contacting me via my official website:anisjerbi.com I will reply to you as soon as possible. If you're a brand and you're interested in sponsoring me, let me know by contacting me via my official website: anisjerbi.com I will respond to your inquiry as soon as I'm able to. Thank you all for all of your support and as always, see you! <3
item.dc:creator: AnisJerbi

item.title: Just don’t, ok?
item.description: This is a long term gripe of mine. Please, pretty please, stop telling women (or anyone) to smile *especially* when we are at work. We’re working. We’re busy. Let us concentrate. We’re not here to be a pretty face for you to look at.
item.dc:creator: forgetwhatisaid

item.title: Raven is totally in character
item.dc:creator: TheOneYouWereWarnedAbout

item.title: I want to not care what they think soooo badly
item.description: I live 8 hours away. I visit 2-3 times a year minimum. They have never been to my current home. I love my life and just want them to be proud of me. But after 45 years as black sheep, I am not optimistic. They are staying at a hotel, even though I have extra room. They have always been incredibly judgy and overbearing. My kid is old enough to sense my stress this time and is loaded for bear. But we've told her that it's not her problem to solve and to be polite. I also told her I'd never not visit her. Been busting ass getting everything cleaned up and ready. I had surgery earlier this summer and things went to the wayside. Ready or not, here they come! I just keep reminding myself IM proud of me. I love MY home. I am giving this visit a fair shot, but also ready for utter disappointment. Edit: Awwww guys!! I'm so overwhelmed by your support! Thank you all! I will update! Gonna be an interesting 4 days!
item.dc:creator: Teachur

item.dc:creator: Quartz87

item.title: So my mom calls me, "This Social Security email form won't download!"
item.description: I remote to her pc, she's in her email and looking at something that SAYS it's from Social Security yet the email address clearly isn't. It's got an .exe attachment. As I watch, she clicks on the attachment and it downloads. Before I can say "Wait!" she clicks on the downloaded attachment and says, "See!" Not that it mattered, she'd obviously done it before. Checked startup programs and uninstalled a VPN, killed some kind of chat client that looked like it was running in the system tray(multiple times) and ran a virus scan. Cleared out temp files/cookies and scanned the registry. She already lost her Facebook account to some guy, God knows what that guy is doing with her account much less all her chat history. Facebook was useless in recovering her account. They were all, "The recovery phone number and email address were changed, those are the only methods we can use to recover your account"
item.dc:creator: RenaissanceFaireMan

item.dc:creator: TheWoodsAreVile

item.title: Potatoes boiled in butter, a splash of vinegar, some water and lots of salt.
item.description: Slowed down and filmed in portrait for your, yes your, viewing pleasure. These were then sliced thinly and placed on some white bread with some butter, cheese, creamy scrambled eggs prepared with some of the boiling liquid an another little lump of butter, some onions pickled with fennel and dill and topped with heaps of Swedish ansjovis.  A cold beer on the side and the ambulance on speed dial. #foodporn #slowedbecauseslut
item.dc:creator: RealityInflicted

item.title: Good morning!
item.description: Still dealing with the aftermath of Hurricane Beryl. I've got no power, internet, or cellular service at home, but I'm not gonna let that stop me from posting.
item.dc:creator: WazertFromBlueSky

item.title: Insert Cybertruck joke here ___ .
item.description: this reminds me of the old compass joke, "why does a compass have a mirror?", So you can see who's lost. source: https://www.popsci.com/technology/mirror-cybertruck/
